label: Performance
summary: General performance patterns outside the hot-path packs.

rules.label: Blocking Sync I/O in Async
rules.description: Synchronous network, subprocess, sleep, or file I/O calls made from async def functions.
rules.explanation: In Python, Blocking Sync I/O in Async matters because the pattern here, Synchronous network, subprocess, sleep, or file I/O calls made from async def functions, creates lifecycle behavior that is easy to miss until the system is under load or shutting down.
rules.fix: For Blocking Sync I/O in Async in Python, give the work an explicit owner, pass the right context or handle, and make cancellation or shutdown visible in the API.

rules.label: Deque Candidate Queue
rules.description: Queue-style list operations like pop(0) or insert(0, ...) that may want collections.deque.
rules.explanation: In Python, Deque Candidate Queue matters because the pattern here, Queue-style list operations like pop(0) or insert(0, ...) that may want collections.deque, is worth review because a few avoidable data-shape or allocation choices can dominate runtime costs in Python.
rules.fix: For Deque Candidate Queue in Python, choose the cheaper structure early, avoid repeated conversions, and reserve broad materialization for places where it is actually needed.

rules.label: Full Dataset Load
rules.description: Calls that load an entire payload into memory instead of streaming.
rules.explanation: In Python, Full Dataset Load matters because the pattern here, Calls that load an entire payload into memory instead of streaming, pushes data or model work into a shape that is harder to scale, cache, or reproduce.
rules.fix: For Full Dataset Load in Python, move expensive data or model work to the right pipeline stage, cache stable results, and keep the runtime path lean.

rules.label: List Materialization First Element
rules.description: list(...)[0] style access that materializes a whole list just to read the first element.
rules.explanation: In Python, List Materialization First Element matters because the pattern here, list(...)[0] style access that materializes a whole list just to read the first element, is worth review because a few avoidable data-shape or allocation choices can dominate runtime costs in Python.
rules.fix: For List Materialization First Element in Python, choose the cheaper structure early, avoid repeated conversions, and reserve broad materialization for places where it is actually needed.

rules.label: List Membership in Loop
rules.description: Repeated membership checks against obviously list-like containers inside loops.
rules.explanation: In Python, List Membership in Loop matters because the pattern here, Repeated membership checks against obviously list-like containers inside loops, pushes avoidable work into a repeated path where the cost compounds quickly.
rules.fix: For List Membership in Loop in Python, move the one-time work out of the repeated path, cache reusable state, or batch the operation so the loop only does per-item work.

rules.label: Recursive Traversal Risk
rules.description: Direct recursion in traversal-style helpers that may be safer as iterative walks for deep inputs.
rules.explanation: In Python, Recursive Traversal Risk matters because the pattern here, Direct recursion in traversal-style helpers that may be safer as iterative walks for deep inputs, is worth review because a few avoidable data-shape or allocation choices can dominate runtime costs in Python.
rules.fix: For Recursive Traversal Risk in Python, choose the cheaper structure early, avoid repeated conversions, and reserve broad materialization for places where it is actually needed.

rules.label: Repeated Len in Loop
rules.description: Repeated len(...) checks inside loops when the receiver appears unchanged locally.
rules.explanation: In Python, Repeated Len in Loop matters because the pattern here, Repeated len(...) checks inside loops when the receiver appears unchanged locally, pushes avoidable work into a repeated path where the cost compounds quickly.
rules.fix: For Repeated Len in Loop in Python, move the one-time work out of the repeated path, cache reusable state, or batch the operation so the loop only does per-item work.

rules.label: String Concat in Loop
rules.description: Repeated string concatenation inside loops can create O(n^2) growth and extra allocations.
rules.explanation: In Python, String Concat in Loop matters because the pattern here, Repeated string concatenation inside loops can create O(n^2) growth and extra allocations, pushes avoidable work into a repeated path where the cost compounds quickly.
rules.fix: For String Concat in Loop in Python, move the one-time work out of the repeated path, cache reusable state, or batch the operation so the loop only does per-item work.

rules.label: Temporary Collection in Loop
rules.description: Loop-local list, dict, or set construction that likely adds avoidable allocation churn.
rules.explanation: In Python, Temporary Collection in Loop matters because the pattern here, Loop-local list, dict, or set construction that likely adds avoidable allocation churn, turns local work into repeated cost that spreads with traffic or input size.
rules.fix: For Temporary Collection in Loop in Python, move one-time initialization, parsing, or allocation outside the loop when the per-iteration state does not actually change.
